name: Core Engine
theme: Game loop, canvas rendering, tile system, physics, camera with parallax scrolling

criteria.description: Opening index.html in a browser displays an HTML5 Canvas element that fills the browser window
testSteps: Open index.html in the browser
Verify a <canvas> element exists on the page
Verify the canvas is visible and has non-zero dimensions
Verify the canvas scales to fill the viewport while preserving aspect ratio
criteria.threshold: Canvas element is present, visible, and scaled to fit the window

criteria.description: The canvas uses 960x540 logical pixels as its internal resolution
testSteps: Open index.html
Inspect the canvas element's width and height attributes (not CSS dimensions)
Verify canvas.width === 960 and canvas.height === 540
criteria.threshold: Canvas internal resolution is exactly 960x540

criteria.description: A fixed-timestep game loop runs at 60 updates per second with rendering interpolation
testSteps: Open the game and let it run for 2 seconds
Check that the game exposes or logs a frame counter or update count
Verify approximately 120 updates have occurred (60/sec × 2 sec, ±10%)
Verify requestAnimationFrame is used for rendering
criteria.threshold: Game loop runs at approximately 60 updates/sec with delta-time driven rendering

criteria.description: The game renders without errors and produces visible output on the canvas
testSteps: Open index.html
Wait 1 second for the game to initialize
Take a screenshot of the canvas
Verify the canvas is not blank (not entirely one color)
Check the browser console for JavaScript errors
criteria.threshold: Canvas shows rendered content with no JS errors in the console

criteria.description: A test level is defined as a 2D array of tile IDs with 32x32 pixel tiles
testSteps: Search the codebase for level/tile data definitions
Verify levels are stored as 2D arrays of numeric tile IDs
Verify TILE_SIZE or equivalent constant equals 32
Verify the test level is at least 80 tiles wide (wider than the viewport)
criteria.threshold: Level data exists as a 2D array with 32px tiles, wider than one screen

criteria.description: Multiple tile types are implemented: solid, one-way platform, hazard, breakable, bounce pad
testSteps: Search for tile type definitions in the codebase
Verify at least 5 distinct tile types are defined (solid, one-way, hazard, breakable, bounce)
Verify each tile type has a unique ID/constant
Verify tiles render with distinct visual appearances
criteria.threshold: At least 5 tile types defined with unique IDs and distinct visuals

criteria.description: Only tiles visible in the viewport are rendered (culling optimization)
testSteps: Review the tile rendering code
Verify the render function calculates visible tile bounds based on camera position
Verify tiles outside the viewport bounds are not drawn
The rendering loop should iterate only over visible column/row ranges
criteria.threshold: Tile rendering uses viewport culling — only visible tiles are drawn each frame

criteria.description: Tiles render with the Forest world's distinct color palette and visual patterns
testSteps: Open the game and view the test level
Take a screenshot showing rendered tiles
Verify tiles use the Forest palette colors (deep greens, browns) not plain colors
Verify solid tiles have texture/pattern (not flat fills)
criteria.threshold: Tiles render with Forest-themed colors and visual texture, not plain flat rectangles

criteria.description: A test entity (player placeholder) is affected by gravity and falls until hitting solid tiles
testSteps: Open the game
Observe the test entity's initial position
Wait a short time and verify the entity has moved downward (gravity)
Verify the entity stops falling when it contacts a solid tile
Verify the entity rests on top of the tile, not inside it
criteria.threshold: Entity falls under gravity and lands on solid tiles without clipping through

criteria.description: AABB collision detection prevents the entity from passing through solid tiles in all directions
testSteps: Move the entity left into a solid wall — verify it stops and doesn't clip through
Move the entity right into a solid wall — verify it stops
Jump the entity into a solid ceiling — verify it bonks and falls back down
Let the entity fall onto a solid floor — verify it lands on top
criteria.threshold: Entity cannot pass through solid tiles in any direction (left, right, up, down)

criteria.description: Gravity applies with terminal velocity — falling speed caps at a maximum
testSteps: Review the physics update code
Verify gravity is applied as a constant acceleration each frame
Verify a terminal velocity constant exists
Verify downward velocity is clamped to terminal velocity
criteria.threshold: Gravity accelerates the entity downward with velocity capped at terminal velocity

criteria.description: One-way platforms allow the entity to jump through from below but land on from above
testSteps: Position the entity below a one-way platform
Make the entity jump upward through it — verify it passes through
After passing above the platform, verify the entity lands on top of it when falling back down
Verify the platform does not block horizontal movement
criteria.threshold: Entity passes through one-way platforms from below but lands on them from above

criteria.description: Moving platforms carry the entity — both horizontal and vertical movement
testSteps: Place the entity on a horizontally moving platform
Verify the entity moves with the platform without drifting off
Place the entity on a vertically moving platform
Verify the entity rides up and down with it without falling through or floating
criteria.threshold: Entity is carried by moving platforms in both horizontal and vertical axes

criteria.description: Ground and air physics differ: ground has higher acceleration and friction, air has lower acceleration and drag
testSteps: Review physics constants for ground vs air states
Verify ground acceleration is higher than air acceleration
Verify ground friction exists and slows the entity when not pressing a direction
Verify air drag is lower than ground friction (less stopping power in air)
criteria.threshold: Distinct ground/air acceleration and friction constants with ground > air acceleration

criteria.description: Arrow keys move the test entity left and right on the ground
testSteps: Open the game and wait for the entity to be on ground
Press the left arrow key — verify entity moves left
Press the right arrow key — verify entity moves right
Release both keys — verify entity decelerates and stops (friction)
criteria.threshold: Left/right arrow keys move the entity horizontally with visible deceleration on release

criteria.description: A jump key (Z) makes the entity jump upward when on the ground
testSteps: Ensure the entity is on the ground
Press the Z key
Verify the entity moves upward (y position decreases)
Verify the entity falls back down due to gravity
Verify the entity cannot jump again while in the air (no infinite jump)
criteria.threshold: Z key causes the entity to jump from the ground and return via gravity; no air-jumping

criteria.description: The camera smoothly follows the entity horizontally as it moves through the level
testSteps: Move the entity to the right for several seconds
Verify the viewport scrolls to follow the entity
Verify new tiles appear on the right side of the screen as the entity moves
Verify the camera movement is smooth (not snapping frame-to-frame)
criteria.threshold: Camera smoothly tracks the entity horizontally, scrolling the level as it moves

criteria.description: Vertical camera tracking uses a dead zone — camera only moves when entity is near top or bottom 25%
testSteps: Review the camera update code
Verify a vertical dead zone is defined (top 25% and bottom 25% thresholds)
Verify the camera does not move vertically when the entity is in the middle 50%
Verify the camera adjusts vertically when the entity moves outside the dead zone
criteria.threshold: Camera stays still vertically when entity is in center 50%, scrolls when in outer 25%

criteria.description: Parallax scrolling background renders at least 3 layers that scroll at different speeds
testSteps: Open the game and take a screenshot showing the background
Move the entity to the right for 2+ seconds
Take another screenshot
Verify background layers are visible behind the tiles
Verify layers move at different rates (far layers slower, near layers faster)
criteria.threshold: At least 3 parallax background layers visible, scrolling at different rates

criteria.description: Camera is clamped to level bounds — does not scroll past the start or end of the level
testSteps: Move the entity to the far left edge of the level
Verify the camera does not scroll past the left boundary (no empty space on the left)
Move the entity to the far right edge
Verify the camera does not scroll past the right boundary
criteria.threshold: Camera stops at level boundaries and does not show empty space beyond the level

criteria.description: The game maintains 60fps during normal gameplay with no dropped frames
testSteps: Open the game and let it run for 5 seconds with the entity moving
Measure frame times using performance.now() or requestAnimationFrame timestamps
Verify the average frame time is ~16.67ms (60fps)
Verify no frame exceeds 33ms (no dropped frames)
criteria.threshold: Average FPS >= 55 over a 5-second period with no individual frame exceeding 33ms

criteria.description: The project uses vanilla JavaScript with no external libraries or dependencies
testSteps: Review all JavaScript source files
Verify no import statements reference node_modules or CDN URLs
Verify no <script> tags load external libraries
Verify no package.json with runtime dependencies exists
criteria.threshold: Zero external runtime dependencies — pure vanilla JS, Canvas API, and Web Audio API only

criteria.description: Code is organized into modular files — separate modules for game loop, tiles, physics, camera, input, and rendering
testSteps: Review the project file structure
Verify separate files/modules exist for major systems (game loop, tiles, physics, camera, input)
Verify modules export/share functionality via a clear pattern (ES modules, namespace objects, or similar)
Verify index.html loads all necessary scripts
criteria.threshold: At least 5 separate JS files/modules for distinct engine systems

criteria.description: The test level includes all tile types in a playable layout — ground, platforms, walls, one-way platforms, hazards, and a gap to traverse
testSteps: Open the game and visually inspect the test level
Verify solid ground tiles form a walkable surface
Verify walls exist that block horizontal movement
Verify one-way platforms are visible and accessible
Verify a hazard tile is present (visually distinct, e.g. red spikes)
Verify the level has a gap that requires jumping to cross
criteria.threshold: Test level contains solid, wall, one-way, hazard tiles, and at least one gap requiring a jump

criteria.description: Hazard tiles deal damage or trigger a reaction when the entity contacts them
testSteps: Move the entity into a hazard tile (spikes or lava)
Verify the entity takes damage, gets knocked back, respawns, or reacts visibly
Verify the hazard is visually distinct from safe tiles
criteria.threshold: Contact with hazard tiles produces a visible gameplay reaction (damage, knockback, or respawn)

criteria.description: Bounce pads launch the entity upward with a higher-than-normal jump impulse
testSteps: Position the entity above a bounce pad tile
Let the entity fall onto the bounce pad
Verify the entity is launched upward higher than a regular jump
Verify the bounce is visually obvious (entity moves significantly upward)
criteria.threshold: Bounce pads launch the entity to a height clearly greater than a normal jump

criteria.description: The test entity (player placeholder) is drawn using Canvas drawing commands — not a plain rectangle
testSteps: Open the game and take a screenshot
Inspect the entity visually — verify it is drawn with some character detail (head, body, limbs) using Canvas shapes
Verify it uses the player color (#3A7BD5 blue body) from the design spec
Verify it is not a plain single-color rectangle
criteria.threshold: Entity is drawn as a recognizable character figure using Canvas shapes, not a plain box

criteria.description: The entity visually faces the direction of movement (flips when changing direction)
testSteps: Move the entity to the right — take a screenshot
Move the entity to the left — take a screenshot
Compare: verify the entity's facing direction changes (sprite flipped)
criteria.threshold: Entity sprite flips horizontally when the movement direction changes

outOfScope: Full player animation states (walk cycle, run cycle, etc.) — Sprint 2
Player combat mechanics (attack, charge attack, health/lives) — Sprint 2
Game state machine (title screen, menus, pause) — Sprint 3
HUD elements (health hearts, coin counter) — Sprint 3
Enemy AI and boss fights — Sprint 5
Particle effects and screen shake — Sprint 11
Wall slide and wall jump mechanics — Sprint 2
Coyote time and jump buffering — Sprint 2
notes: This sprint establishes the engine foundation. The test entity is a player placeholder that can move and jump but does not have the full player movement vocabulary (wall jump, coyote time, combat, etc.). The focus is on rock-solid physics, tile rendering, camera, and game loop — the systems everything else builds on.
